title: ocotea adela
intro: Ocotea adela, also known as the Oreganillo tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Ocotea adela plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds such as caryophyllene and limonene, with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ocotea adela include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting the immune system. Ocotea adela is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be consumed or applied topically to reap its benefits.
